summary: electron-orbit exfiltrates environment variables to Azure Blob endpoint via obfuscated postinstall + require-time payload
details: The package performs two-stage exfiltration of the installer's environment variables. On npm install, the postinstall hook (install.js, wired via package.json scripts.postinstall) enumerates process.env, filters out PATH-like keys, and writes the JSON dump to os.tmpdir()/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt. On first require of dist/index.js, the marker file is read back, XOR-obfuscated with the string 'electron-orbit', hex-encoded, and appended as the 'version=' query parameter of an HTTPS GET to electronorbit.blob.core.windows.net/icons/orbit.svg (SAS-signed), delivered through a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client that bypasses node:https. Both files use an XOR-string decoder (__s/l helpers) to hide the identifiers 'process', 'env', 'node:net', 'connect', the marker filename, the destination hostname, the HTTP request line, and the Host header. A gating check computes sha256(process.env.BuildType) and only assembles the real exfil URL when the digest matches a hardcoded prefix — otherwise the icon source is set to '%TEMP%' and no request fires, so casual review environments see no traffic. Package metadata impersonates the OrbitDB ecosystem (name 'electron-orbit', author 'OrbitDB Archive', homepage github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron) despite having no functional relationship to OrbitDB. CI/CD secrets, cloud tokens, and personal configuration routinely present in process.env are captured in the destination storage account's access logs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
